results.title: LA Kings’ Black, Silver & Bold Program Concludes 2025 -26 Season, Expanding Access and Driving Measurable Impact in Youth Hockey
results.description: On Wednesday, June 17, 2026, AEG ’s LA Kings concluded the 2025–26 season of Black, Silver & Bold, a year-long initiative focused on expanding access, advancing development, and fostering inclusion in youth hockey across Los Angeles. Led by LA Kings Inclusion Specialist Blake Bolden, the program, which is offered at no cost to participants, delivered a comprehensive experience for 68 youth participants combining on-ice training with mentorship and leadership development opportunities. Designed to support underrepresented elite players, Black Silver & Bold continues to build a more inclusive pipeline for the next generation of hockey talent. The program, now in its third year, achieved significant growth and measurable impact. Participation increased from 55 athletes in 2024–25 to 68 in 2025–26. Notably, representation among girls rose from 13 to 40 athletes, an increase of more than 200%, underscoring the program’s continued commitment to broadening access and opportunity across the sport. Throughout the season, participants took part in immersive experiences, including: On-ice training sessions led by Kings coaches and hockey professionals Behind-the-scenes access to LA Kings games and facilities Postgame meet-and-greet opportunities with NHL players and industry leaders Community service initiatives including food and toy drives that reinforced the program’s emphasis on leadership and giving back By removing financial barriers and creating direct access to elite training, mentorship, and career pathways, Black, Silver & Bold continues to deliver tangible outcomes both on and off the ice. As the program concludes its 2025-26 season, the LA Kings remain committed to scaling its impact, ensuring that access, representation and opportunity in hockey continue to expand across Los Angeles communities. results.title: WIAA board approves limited use of shot clock for girls and boys basketball starting this season
results.description: STEVENS POINT — The WIAA Board of Control approved limited use of the shot clock in basketball in 2026-27 and acted on other recommendations from the Winter Coaches Advisory Committees affecting season regulations and membership policies at its June meeting...

results.title: Reason behind Fifita’s absence at training revealed; Star Tiger on track for early return — Late Mail
results.description: South Sydney Rabbitohs coach Wayne Bennett has confirmed that David Fifita will “play” on Thursday night, he has provided the reason why he missed the club’s captain’s run on Wednesday.

results.title: ‘I Didn’t Deserve It’ — Jude Bellingham Says Man of the Match Award Belonged to Ghana Player After England Draw at FIFA World Cup 2026
results.description: Jude Bellingham praised Ghana’s defensive display after England were held to a 0-0 draw at the FIFA World Cup 2026, insisting a Ghana player deserved the Man of the Match award instead of him following England’s frustrating group-stage result. The post ‘I Didn’t Deserve It’ — Jude Bellingham Says Man of the Match Award Belonged to Ghana Player After England Draw at FIFA World Cup 2026 appeared first on The Sunday Guardian .

results.title: 'VAR went for a coffee' — Carlos Queiroz fumes at penalty snub as tactical Ghana hold England
results.description: Analysis: Despite commanding 79% of the ball, a wasteful England side miss the chance to seal early World Cup progression as Harry Kane lets a late winner slip through his fingers.

results.title: Ryan Johnson dominates, Nolan Schanuel adds 2-run HR as Angels beat Orioles 5-1
results.description: Rookie right-hander Ryan Johnson gave up one hit over six scoreless innings, Nolan Schanuel hit an early two-run home run and the Los Angeles Angels earned a 5-1 victory over the Baltimore Orioles. In his third career start, Johnson (1-2)...
